# Research - 2026-07-19

Focus: **all** (CLI, providers, tools, MCP, commands, hooks, skills, plugins)

> Sunday, `date -u +%u` = 7. Weekly repos (claude-code, aider) skipped per
> cadence rule. Cline shipped optional peer dependencies for provider
> packages (#12379, ~250MB install size savings), team spawning fixes
> (#12371, #12369, #12370: OAuth token refresh, error reporting, tool
> visibility), API credit error detection (#12394), and Kimi K3 model
> support (#12392). Kilocode and OpenCode show only minor commits or items
> already covered in yesterday's brief (custom file extensions #12306,
> sidebar usage #12303, UTF-8 BOM #12277, shell resolution #12331, UI
> components #12374). Zero open `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). Most
> new commits are releases, dependency management, or internal plumbing;
> one actionable feature (#12379 optional provider peers) is applicable to
> Alexi but LOW priority (Alexi has only one provider today, future-facing).

## Executive Summary

- **#1 (LOW, ~4h, provider)**: Cline #12379 moved Claude Code and Codex
  provider native binaries (~250MB claude-agent-sdk, ~105MB @openai/codex)
  to optional peerDependencies with dynamic imports, saving install size
  for users who never select those providers. Port to Alexi: Alexi
  currently has only one provider (SapOrchestrationProvider via SAP AI
  Core). The pattern is applicable if Alexi ever adds native-binary
  providers (e.g. local Ollama, native Claude Code SDK) in the future:
  make them optional peers, dynamic-import via literal `import()`, and
  provide graceful degradation (npx fallback or user-installed PATH
  resolution). Low value today (no multi-provider native binaries yet, no
  immediate install-size pain); document for future reference. Estimated
  ~4h if/when needed: move provider to optionalPeerDependencies (~30m),
  add dynamic import with fallback (~2h), test install/fallback scenarios
  (~1.5h).
- **#2 (INFRASTRUCTURE, NOT PORTABLE)**: Cline #12371, #12369, #12370
  fixed team spawning (OAuth token refresh for teammate/subagent sessions,
  error reporting for failed runs, hiding team_spawn tool from non-lead
  teammates). Alexi does not implement multi-agent teams, teammates, or
  lead/delegate spawning. The OAuth token refresh pattern (onAuthError
  hook, RuntimeOAuthTokenManager) is VS Code extension + Cline API
  specific. Not portable. Skip.
- **#3 (LOW, ~2h, provider)**: Cline #12394 improved API credit error
  detection. The CLI now detects real `insufficient_credits` errors from
  the Cline API (previously missed due to error-response shape mismatch).
  Port to Alexi: Alexi uses SAP AI Core, not Cline API, so the exact
  error shape differs. The *principle* (parse provider-specific credit/
  quota errors and surface them clearly instead of generic network
  failures) is applicable. Alexi already has error handling in
  `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` (inferred from provider structure).
  Review for SAP AI Core quota errors (HTTP 429, `ResourceExhausted`,
  deployment quota exceeded). Low value (edge case, most users have
  sufficient quota). Estimated ~2h: review SAP AI Core error responses
  (~30m), add quota error detection (~1h), test (~30m).
- **#4 (LOW, ~1h, provider)**: Cline #12392 added Kimi K3 to ClinePass
  model catalog with curated ordering (newest models don't auto-become
  default; authored order is preserved). Port to Alexi: Alexi's model
  catalog is derived from SAP AI Core deployments (no hard-coded model
  list). The *principle* (preserve authored/curated order for defaults
  instead of auto-sorting by release date) is applicable if Alexi ever
  adds a curated model catalog UI or default-model selection logic. Low
  value today (no curated catalog, `getDefaultModel` uses routing config
  or first available). Document for future reference. Estimated ~1h if
  needed: add `authoredOrder` field to catalog schema, apply to default
  selection.
- **Rotation**: kilocode 1 dryness (zero new actionable items today, but
  1 < 5 threshold, keep daily), cline 0 dryness (active), opencode 1
  dryness (zero new actionable items today, keep daily). Weekly repos not
  polled (Sunday). No demotions.

## Detailed Findings

#### 1. Optional peer dependencies for provider packages (provider, ~4h, LOW)

- **What**: Cline #12379 moved Claude Code and Codex provider packages to
  optional peerDependencies. The `@cline/llms` package previously
  hard-depended on `ai-sdk-provider-claude-code` (bundles ~250MB
  claude-agent-sdk platform binary) and `ai-sdk-provider-codex-cli`
  (bundles ~105MB @openai/codex). Every `npm install cline` pulled both
  native binaries even for users who never select those providers (90%+
  of installs per Cline telemetry, inferred from PR body). The fix moves
  both to `optionalPeerDependencies` (kept as `devDependencies` so
  monorepo builds still bundle the JS) and loads them via literal dynamic
  imports in `community.ts` (similar to the existing opencode-sdk
  pattern). The Claude Code provider now resolves the `claude` executable
  explicitly: bundled platform package when present (`require.resolve`
  check), otherwise user-installed `claude` from PATH, passed via
  `defaultSettings.pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`. The Codex provider
  already had graceful degradation (`npx -y @openai/codex`, then `codex`
  on PATH). The PR notes that the agent SDK's own executable resolution
  cannot be used from Bun-compiled binaries because it anchors on the
  virtual bunfs where `node_modules` lookups never see packages on disk
  (so explicit PATH resolution is required for standalone builds). Result:
  users who never select Claude Code or Codex save ~355MB on install;
  users who do select them get prompted to install the peer or fall back
  to `npx`/PATH.
- **Why**: Alexi currently has only one provider: `SapOrchestrationProvider`
  (`src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`), which talks to SAP AI Core via
  HTTPS (no native binaries, no bundled SDKs). The pattern is applicable
  if Alexi ever adds native-binary providers in the future (e.g. local
  Ollama with platform-specific libtorch, native Claude Code SDK, local
  Codex). Port to Alexi when that happens: move the provider package to
  `optionalPeerDependencies`, dynamic-import via literal `import(...)`,
  provide graceful degradation (npx fallback, user-installed PATH
  resolution, or a clear error message). The explicit executable
  resolution (check bundled package first, then PATH) is the key insight
  for Bun-compiled / single-binary CLI scenarios. Low value today (no
  multi-provider native binaries yet, no immediate install-size pain).
  Document for future reference. Estimated ~4h when needed: move provider
  to optionalPeerDependencies (~30m), add dynamic import with
  try/catch/fallback (~2h), test install scenarios (peer present, peer
  missing, npx fallback, PATH fallback, Bun-compiled build) (~1.5h).
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12379 "feat(llms): make Claude Code and
  Codex provider packages optional peers" (merged 2026-07-19T04:12Z,
  commit `3f7a9e1`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12379.
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: moderate (~4h: move to optional
  peers, dynamic import, fallback, test install scenarios — when
  native-binary providers are added).
- **Status**: LOW (not applicable today, Alexi has only one provider and
  no native binaries; document for future reference when adding Ollama /
  Claude Code SDK / local model providers).

#### 2. Team spawning OAuth/error handling fixes (tool, INFRASTRUCTURE, NOT PORTABLE)

- **What**: Cline #12371, #12369, #12370 fixed team spawning
  (multi-teammate orchestration). Three related fixes: (1) OAuth token
  refresh for teammate and subagent sessions (#12369). Teammate sessions
  inherit the OAuth access token as a snapshot at spawn time and
  previously had no refresh path; when the token expired mid-turn (e.g.
  while the lead was blocked in `team_await_runs`), their next model call
  died with the provider's raw 401 body. The fix adds an `onAuthError`
  hook to `AgentConfig`, wired by `LocalRuntimeHost`; it refreshes
  credentials through the shared single-flight
  `RuntimeOAuthTokenManager` and propagates the new key to the lead,
  delegated defaults, and all teammates via existing `updateConnection`
  channels. `SessionRuntime` retries a run once when it failed with an
  auth-like error and the refresh succeeded (continuing from the
  persisted trail so completed iterations aren't replayed). Also fixes
  `isLikelyAuthError` to lowercase string inputs (the server's
  'Unauthorized: ...' message only matched when wrapped in an Error). (2)
  Report errored teammate runs as failed instead of completed (#12370).
  Model-stream failures return results with `finishReason: 'error'`
  rather than throwing, so `executeQueuedRun` marked such runs
  'completed' with the error buried in `resultSummary`. The fix throws
  into the existing failure path so the run reports `status: 'failed'`
  (with `run.error` set and a `RunFailed` event) and the retry machinery
  engages when `maxRetries` allows. (3) Hide `team_spawn_teammate` tool
  from teammates (#12371). Spawning is lead-only, enforced at execution
  time, so teammates that saw the tool in their toolset burned turns on
  'Only the lead agent can manage teammates.' rejections before falling
  back to doing the work themselves. The fix removes the spawn tool from
  teammate tool configurations.
- **Why**: Alexi does not implement multi-agent teams, teammates, or
  lead/delegate spawning. The OAuth token refresh pattern (`onAuthError`
  hook, `RuntimeOAuthTokenManager`, per-session credential propagation)
  is VS Code extension + Cline API specific (Cline has a hub service that
  provides OAuth-backed model access; Alexi uses direct SAP AI Core
  service keys or proxy configs, no OAuth refresh flow). The teammate run
  error reporting and tool visibility fixes are for a team orchestration
  system that Alexi does not have. Not portable. Skip.
- **Source**: cline/cline PRs #12371 "fix(sdk): stop exposing the team
  spawn tool to teammates" (merged 2026-07-19T03:54Z, commit `8b2c7f3`),
  #12370 "fix(sdk): report errored teammate runs as failed instead of
  completed" (merged 2026-07-19T03:51Z, commit `4a1d9e2`), #12369
  "fix(sdk): retry runs once after refreshing expired OAuth credentials"
  (merged 2026-07-19T03:48Z, commit `7e9a3f1`). Full PR bodies at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12371,
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12370,
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12369.
- **Category**: tool. **Size**: N/A (not portable, VS Code + Cline API
  multi-agent team system, Alexi has no teammates or OAuth refresh
  flow).
- **Status**: SKIP (not portable, multi-agent teams not applicable to
  Alexi).

#### 3. Detect real API credit errors (provider, ~2h, LOW)

- **What**: Cline #12394 improved API credit error detection. The CLI now
  detects real `insufficient_credits` errors from the Cline API
  (previously missed due to error-response shape mismatch: the API
  returns `{ error: { code: 'insufficient_credits', ... } }`, but the
  client checked only top-level `error.code`, not nested). The fix parses
  the nested error shape and surfaces a clear "Insufficient credits"
  message instead of a generic network failure. Part of Cline's hosted
  ClinePass model access (users buy credits, API enforces quotas).
- **Why**: Alexi uses SAP AI Core, not Cline API, so the exact error
  shape differs. SAP AI Core quota errors are HTTP 429 or
  `ResourceExhausted` gRPC codes (inferred from SAP AI SDK docs, not read
  in code). The *principle* (parse provider-specific credit/quota errors
  and surface them clearly instead of generic network failures) is
  applicable. Port to Alexi: review `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts`
  for error handling; check if SAP AI Core quota errors (429,
  ResourceExhausted, deployment quota exceeded) are already parsed and
  surfaced. If not, add detection similar to Cline's nested error check:
  parse the SAP error response, detect quota signals, throw a clear
  `QuotaExceededError` or similar (inherit from base error class) that
  the orchestrator can catch and display as "SAP AI Core quota exceeded
  for deployment X" instead of "Network request failed". Low value (edge
  case, most users have sufficient quota, and SAP AI Core likely already
  surfaces quota errors in HTTP status). Estimated ~2h: review current
  SAP AI Core error responses (~30m), add quota error detection if
  missing (~1h), test with mock 429 response (~30m).
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12394 "fix(cli): detect real
  insufficient_credits error from Cline API" (merged 2026-07-19T05:27Z,
  commit `9a8c2f4`). Full PR body at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12394.
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~2h: review SAP error
  responses, add quota detection if needed, test).
- **Status**: LOW (edge case, most users have sufficient quota; SAP AI
  Core likely already surfaces 429/quota errors in HTTP status; confirm
  current behavior before implementing).

#### 4. Kimi K3 model catalog with curated ordering (provider, ~1h, LOW)

- **What**: Cline #12392 added Kimi K3 to the ClinePass model catalog and
  fixed default model selection to preserve authored/curated order.
  Previously, `firstGeneratedModelId` sorted models by `releaseDate`
  (newest first), so adding a new model with the latest date would flip
  the default for new ClinePass setups. The fix uses the catalog's
  authored order instead (first model in the array is the intended
  default, mirroring the curated order from the ClinePass
  recommended-models endpoint). The commit also adds telemetry for
  auth-run-retry events (from #12369).
- **Why**: Alexi's model catalog is derived from SAP AI Core deployments
  at runtime (query available models via
  `GET /lm/deployments?resourceGroup=...`, inferred from SAP AI SDK
  docs). There is no hard-coded model list in Alexi
  (`src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` constructs the provider for any
  model ID; routing config `routing-config.json` maps task/capability to
  model, but it is user-authored, not auto-generated). The *principle*
  (preserve authored/curated order for defaults instead of auto-sorting
  by release date) is applicable if Alexi ever adds a curated model
  catalog UI (e.g. `alexi models --recommended`) or default-model
  selection logic that ranks models. Port to Alexi if that feature is
  added: include an `authoredOrder` or `priority` field in the catalog
  schema, apply it to default selection instead of release date or
  alphabetical. Low value today (no curated catalog, `getDefaultModel` in
  `src/providers/index.ts` uses routing config or first available model;
  user specifies model explicitly via `-m` flag or config). Document for
  future reference. Estimated ~1h when needed: add `authoredOrder` field
  to catalog schema (~15m), apply to default selection logic (~30m), test
  (~15m).
- **Source**: cline/cline PR #12392 "fix(llms): add Kimi K3 to bundled
  ClinePass model fallback" (merged 2026-07-19T04:58Z, commit `2b8c3f2`).
  Also PR #12393 "docs: add Kimi K3 to ClinePass model list and reference
  pricing" (merged same time, commit `1a9d8e3`). Full PR bodies at
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12392 and
  https://github.com/cline/cline/pull/12393.
- **Category**: provider. **Size**: small (~1h: add authoredOrder field,
  apply to default selection — when curated catalog UI is added).
- **Status**: LOW (no curated catalog today, user specifies model
  explicitly; document for future reference if adding recommended-models
  UI).

## Actionable Recommendations

1. **Priority order**: None of today's items are HIGH or MODERATE. All
   four (#1 optional peer deps, #2 team spawning, #3 API credit errors,
   #4 model catalog ordering) are LOW or SKIP. #1 is future-facing (not
   applicable until Alexi adds native-binary providers), #2 is not
   portable (multi-agent teams), #3 is edge-case (quota errors likely
   already surfaced by SAP AI Core HTTP status), #4 is future-facing (no
   curated catalog today).
2. **Optional peer dependencies (#1)**: Do NOT surface as an issue.
   Document for future reference: when adding native-binary providers
   (Ollama, Claude Code SDK, local models), move them to
   optionalPeerDependencies, dynamic-import, and provide graceful
   degradation (npx fallback, PATH resolution). Reference: Cline #12379.
   Estimated ~4h when needed.
3. **Team spawning (#2)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Not portable (Alexi
   has no multi-agent teams, no OAuth refresh flow). Skip.
4. **API credit errors (#3)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Low value (edge
   case, SAP AI Core likely already surfaces 429/quota errors in HTTP
   status). If quota error reporting becomes a pain point (user reports),
   revisit: review `src/providers/sapOrchestration.ts` error handling,
   add explicit quota error detection. Reference: Cline #12394. Estimated
   ~2h if needed.
5. **Model catalog ordering (#4)**: Do NOT surface as an issue. Document
   for future reference: if adding a curated recommended-models UI, use
   authored order instead of release-date sorting for default selection.
   Reference: Cline #12392. Estimated ~1h when needed.
6. **Rotation**: kilocode 1 dryness (zero new actionable items today; 1 <
   5, keep daily), cline 0 dryness (active, four items analyzed), opencode
   1 dryness (zero new actionable items today; 1 < 5, keep daily). Weekly

7. **Observation**: Zero open `auto-implement` issues (unchanged). The
   engineering pipeline remains clear. Today's scan produced zero
   actionable issues (all LOW or SKIP); no new issues to create.
8. **Carry-forward from yesterday**: Yesterday's brief (2026-07-18)
